title: Probem cross-compiling ABC (From Yosys)
body:
I'm trying to cross compile Yosis for Windows using docker and the mxe target;

```
FROM debian:jessie
MAINTAINER Udi Finkelstein <github@udifink.om>
RUN \
  echo "deb http://pkg.mxe.cc/repos/apt/debian jessie main" > /etc/apt/sources.list.d/mxeapt.list && \
  apt-key adv --keyserver keyserver.ubuntu.com --recv-keys D43A795B73B16ABE9643FE1AFD8FFF16DB45C6AB && \
  apt-get update -y && \
  apt-get install -y \
  autoconf \
  bison \
  flex \
  g++ \
  gawk \
  gettext \
  git \
  libffi-dev \
  make \
  mercurial \
  mxe-i686-w64-mingw32.static-gcc \
  pkg-config \
  python3 \
   tcl-dev \
  xdot

RUN git clone https://github.com/cliffordwolf/yosys.git && cd yosys && make config-mxe && \
  ln -s /usr/lib/mxe /usr/local/src/mxe && \
  make mxebin

RUN git clone https://github.com/cliffordwolf/icestorm.git icestorm && cd icestorm && make -j$(nproc) mxebin

RUN git clone https://github.com/cseed/arachne-pnr.git && cd arachne-pnr && make mxebin
```

Compilation progresses fine, until I get the following error while compiling ABC:

```
src/proof/acec/acecSt.o: In function `Z17Acec_StatsCollectP10Gia_Man_t_i':
/yosys/abc/src/proof/acec/acecSt.c:409: undefined reference to `Dar_LibReturnClass(unsigned int)'
collect2: error: ld returned 1 exit status
make[1]: *** [abc-a4872e22c646.exe] Error 1
```

After some investigation it seems that the cross compile configs always use '-x c++' causing all files to be parsed as C++ (despite their '.c' suffix).
The file defining Dar_LibReturnClass() (sorry, forgot the path for this file) is compiled as C, while src/proof/acec/acecSt.c is compiled as C++, assuming Dar_LibReturnClass() is a C++ func and calls it with a mangled filename.
The non-cross-compile versions successfully compile ABC as C code.
This also happened when I tried to use the Ubuntu built-in mingw32 cross compile packages (I created soft links from /usr/bin/mingw... to the hardcoded path in the Yosys Makefile).

I tracked the source of the '-x c++' flag and it dates back to the original commit of the cross-compile code in the Makefile.
Is there any special reason you compiled ABC as C++?
